subject: Mathematics
title: Proof by Induction
description: A short lesson to help you apply Proof by Induction.

hook.text: Prove it for 1. Then prove that if it works for n, it works for n+1. You've proven it for all numbers.

buildup.text: Mathematical induction is like dominoes: knock down the first, and prove each one topples the next.

discovery.text: The base case starts the chain. The inductive step keeps it going forever.

twist.text: Despite its name, mathematical induction is deductive—every step is logically certain.

climax.text: Induction proves statements about all natural numbers in just two steps: base and inductive.

punchline.text: Two steps prove an infinite number of truths.

quiz.question: What are the two parts of a proof by induction?
